assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: Task 2 (Repository: trustify-backend), Task 3 (Repository: trustify-backend), Task 8 (Repository: trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: Task 1 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 4 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 5 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 6 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 7 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 9 (Repository: trustify-ui). Both repositories are targeted with multiple tasks each.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are numbered 2 and 3. Frontend tasks that depend on backend tasks: Task 4 (trustify-ui) depends on Task 3 (trustify-backend). Task 5 depends on Task 4 which depends on Task 3. All frontend tasks that depend on backend work (tasks 4-7) have higher numbers than backend tasks 2-3. Task 8 (backend documentation) has no frontend tasks depending on it, so its higher number does not violate the constraint.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (comparison page) has extensive Figma/PatternFly references in Implementation Notes: 'PatternFly Select (single, typeahead)', 'PatternFly ExpandableSection', 'PatternFly Badge -- green for Added/Resolved, red for Removed/New Vulnerabilities, blue for Version Changes, yellow for License Changes', 'PatternFly Table (composable) -- sortable columns', 'PatternFly EmptyState with CodeBranchIcon', 'PatternFly Skeleton'. Task 6 (export) references 'The Figma design shows an "Export" dropdown (PatternFly Dropdown)'. Task 4 (API types, hook) is API-layer and exempt -- it contains no Figma references as expected.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 4 (trustify-ui) explicitly depends on Task 3 (trustify-backend) in its Dependencies section: 'Depends on: Task 3 -- Add SBOM comparison REST endpoint with integration tests'. Additionally, all intermediate tasks target the shared feature branch TC-9003, and backend tasks (2, 3) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (4, 5, 6, 7).

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task 2 references paths in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/Cargo.toml, common/src/error.rs, entity/src/sbom_package.rs, entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs, entity/src/package_license.rs. Backend task 3 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs. Frontend task 4 references paths in repo-frontend.md: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts, src/api/client.ts. Frontend task 5 references: src/routes.tsx, src/pages/SbomDetailPage/, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/components/EmptyStateCard.tsx. Frontend task 7 references: tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/mocks/fixtures/. All paths correspond to their respective repo manifests.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repo name: Task 1: 'trustify-ui', Task 2: 'trustify-backend', Task 3: 'trustify-backend', Task 4: 'trustify-ui', Task 5: 'trustify-ui', Task 6: 'trustify-ui', Task 7: 'trustify-ui', Task 8: 'trustify-backend', Task 9: 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain all four required sections. Verified by section headers: Task 1 has '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', '## Test Requirements'. Task 2 has '## Target Branch' (TC-9003), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria' (6 items), '## Test Requirements' (6 items). Same pattern confirmed for tasks 3-9. Every task has all four required sections.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths all exist in repo manifests: Task 2 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/Cargo.toml (all in repo-backend.md). Task 3 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs (in repo-backend.md). Task 4 modifies src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts (in repo-frontend.md). Task 5 modifies src/routes.tsx (in repo-frontend.md). Task 7 modifies tests/mocks/handlers.ts (in repo-frontend.md). Files to Create are new files placed in directories that exist in the manifests: e.g., modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs in existing model/ directory, src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts in existing hooks/ directory, src/pages/SbomComparePage/ follows the src/pages/ pattern.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend, non-documentation task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) and documentation tasks are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (bookend) and Task 9 (bookend) have no Implementation Notes -- exempt. Task 8 (documentation) has no Implementation Notes -- exempt. Non-exempt tasks: Task 2 references specific paths: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs for the SbomSummary struct pattern', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs for the SbomService pattern', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 3 references: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs for handler structure', 'common/src/error.rs', 'endpoints/mod.rs'. Task 4 references: 'src/api/models.ts', 'src/api/client.ts', 'src/hooks/useSbomById.ts'. Task 5 references: 'src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx', 'src/pages/SbomDetailPage/', PatternFly component mappings. Task 6 references: 'PatternFly Dropdown component', browser Blob API pattern. Task 7 references: 'src/pages/SbomDetailPage/SbomDetailPage.test.tsx', 'tests/mocks/handlers.ts'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks target 'main': Task 1 (create-branch): Target Branch = main, Task 9 (merge-branch): Target Branch = main. All intermediate tasks target the feature issue ID: Task 2: TC-9003, Task 3: TC-9003, Task 4: TC-9003, Task 5: TC-9003, Task 6: TC-9003, Task 7: TC-9003, Task 8: TC-9003.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have corresponding digest files with valid sha256-md hashes (64 lowercase hex chars each): task-1-create-feature-branch-digest.md: sha256-md:1583033cd0619dacd8d56eaca03e4606102722f9ff9a5dc7814ecb156e0fe19c, task-2-sbom-comparison-model-service-digest.md: sha256-md:b06cbc14c5462a2326650fe29354610064d9f808e4e50fea103fdc9982d2cea0, task-3-sbom-comparison-endpoint-digest.md: sha256-md:84010f27d469df4ef763c119545b157639ef804cb43485186646e5bdd5ca9889, task-4: sha256-md:3c81bec0b06e3c2a766b938bb41b9d857b7a787b6b399942b4ae384dbb15677a, task-5: sha256-md:8571c46951838553e43963ce29935397a1512dd424adb65fe02f97445bc239bc, task-6: sha256-md:223c446fa190346521b25b24fb0a536dfb1db9075f0037851e1e48c7f41000e1, task-7: sha256-md:d13d4835cdef1af05a2ee1e7c03263ec8fe4172d476474f0f96ee028b8ef5afb, task-8: sha256-md:c1fdf5a1b0f2a9b3160189b87194437c66631948cb3bdcafcb2970cd9398836a, task-9: sha256-md:32346fc7d4bea1bcc47b6e45af0455bb66964eacc3d3023929112af839e90c7f. All use the '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<hash>' format.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references in Implementation Notes include applicability rationales in the prescribed format. Task 2: 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs matching the convention's Rust module scope.' and 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/comparison.rs matching the convention's Rust service scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs matching the convention's endpoint registration scope.' and 'Applies: task modifies tests/api/sbom.rs matching the convention's Rust test file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task creates src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts matching the convention's TypeScript hook scope.' and 'Applies: task modifies src/api/rest.ts matching the convention's TypeScript API layer scope.' Tasks 5-7 follow the same pattern. No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. All rationales name specific files and scopes, not free-form prose.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md header states '**Priority**: Critical'. The summary-comment.md documents: '**Priority**: Critical — propagated to all created tasks via `additional_fields: { "priority": {"name": "Critical"} }`'. The additional_fields section in summary-comment.md confirms: '"priority": {"name": "Critical"}' is included in the fields for all created tasks and epics.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md header states '**Fix Versions**: RHTPA 1.5.0'. The summary-comment.md documents: '**fixVersions**: RHTPA 1.5.0 — propagated to all created tasks via `additional_fields: { "fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}] }` (fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults section is configured)'. The CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so the default 'both' applies, and fixVersions are correctly propagated.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The file summary-comment.md exists at /tmp/plan-feature-eval-baseline/eval-3/outputs/summary-comment.md. It contains an 'Inherited Field Values' section documenting: '**Priority**: Critical — propagated to all created tasks via additional_fields' and '**fixVersions**: RHTPA 1.5.0 — propagated to all created tasks via additional_fields (fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults section is configured)'. It also shows the combined additional_fields JSON block with both priority and fixVersions values.
